text:
Olympian Christopher Morales-Williams hit back at his ‘old’ competitors ahead of the 400 metre semi-final later today.

Morales-Williams, who turned 20 earlier this week, booked his spot in the semi-final with a stellar run during Sunday’s heat.

The Canadian finished second in his qualification run, completing one lap of the athletics track in just under 45 seconds.

However, when quizzed on his performance, Morales-Williams revealed competing at the Olympics wasn’t even the hardest thing he’d ever done.

Speaking after the event, the 400m runner said: “I was talking with Coach Caryl [Smith-Gilbert], she was telling me ‘You’ve done harder things than this already.’ SECs [the US college championship] was harder.

“SECs last year was harder than any race I’ve done this year. The hardest part was just getting to be that fast.”

Morales-Williams is right to reference the SECs, given he recorded the fastest 400-metre time in the world this year at the event earlier this year.

The University of Georgia runner went at 44.05 seconds - a time that could see him challenge for a medal in Paris.

Meanwhile...one of the funniest interviews of the Olympics so far was when NCAA champion Christopher Morales-Williams of@UGATrackwas asked about handling pressure at the Olympics. He's 19 years old and representing Canada and just advanced to the semifinal.Here's what he…pic.twitter.com/jbFxznPfS9



But when speaking about what he plans to do if the semi-final doesn’t go his way, Morales-Williams added: “Now I just go out there and have fun. If I lose, I just go back to school and continue on with my life.

“But these guys, you know, they’re old. They’ve got cars, and kids. I don’t even own a car, I don’t even have to pay for gas.

“So it’s like, what do I even need to worry about? Spending money on pencils?”

Despite the coy approach, Morales-Williams will be hoping to impress when he returns to the track for the 400-metre semis.

Don't miss:'Genius' reason Olympians wear all black clothes when competing

Don't miss:Luana Alonso breaks her silence after claims she was 'inappropriate' at Olympics

Don't miss:Gabby Logan issues sad health update after suffering 'brain fog' at Olympics

He’s been placed in a semi-final with Michael Norman, who placed fifth in the 400 metres at the last Games.

The British and European record holder Matthew Hudson-Smith will also take to the blocks in Morales-Williams’ heat.

Hudson-Smith boasts a personal best of 43.74, which he recorded at the London Diamon League meeting last month.

Morales-Williams and co. are due to set off in their semi-final at 6:49 PM BST.

It's already been a hectic day on the track, withthree false starts recorded in one of the earlier 110m hurdle heats.

title: Olympic star trolls 'old' opponents before returning to school in brutal put-down | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Amos Murphy
description: Christopher Morales-Williams had some harsh words for his 'older' competitors at the Paris Games.
